chief_complaint: A 71-year-old man with type 2 diabetes presents with acute kidney injury.
history: The patient developed oliguric AKI one month after initiating dapagliflozin. He experienced transient appetite loss associated with a COVID-19 infection. He has a history of chronic kidney disease (CKD).
exam: Vital signs: not available. Physical examination: not available.
labs: Serum creatinine: 2.0 mg/dL (initial), 8.3 mg/dL (peak), Kidney biopsy: characteristic features of osmotic nephropathy.
correct_diagnosis: Osmotic nephropathy as a complication of SGLT2 inhibitor therapy
differentials: Osmotic nephropathy as a complication of SGLT2 inhibitor therapy
Prerenal AKI due to COVID-19 infection
correct_ranking: Osmotic nephropathy as a complication of SGLT2 inhibitor therapy
Prerenal AKI due to COVID-19 infection
